content: LightRAG's dual-level hierarchical knowledge graph retrieval can be repurposed as a dynamic working memory management system for autonomous agents that inherently offsets the constraint of fixed working memory capacity limits. By offloading low-level detail to a persistent shared knowledge graph and only pulling high-level relational structure and contextually relevant low-level facts into the agent's active context window during reasoning, agents can maintain coherent long-horizon reasoning without hitting working memory capacity caps.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
